mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines faster than near-term cash flows adjust, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive 20-day returns and rejected when easing episodes do not outperform unchanged-policy periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising months, or the triggered hit rate stays below 55% in rolling out-of-sample tests, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weaker household income and demand, which depresses earnings expectations and pushes the S&P 500 lower over the following month. The effect is strongest when the unemployment move is abrupt rather than gradual.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more often than negative ones, or if the average forward return after triggers exceeds the unconditional mean in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and better revenue momentum for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should weaken when sales growth is already fully priced or when the series is distorted by one-off spending spikes.
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not produce higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the return relation flips for more than one consecutive 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL growth stays contained, it indicates improving real output without immediate inflation pressure, which supports higher equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if industrial gains are accompanied by accelerating inflation that forces tighter policy.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising INDPRO and subdued CPIAUCSL do not outperform the unconditional SPX 60-day return distribution, or if the joint signal has negative information ratio in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST marks weakening housing demand and rising cyclical stress, which tends to precede broader equity weakness over the next month. The signal is strongest when housing starts are falling from already elevated levels and weakest when they rebound after a weather disruption.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 10% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below zero across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE signals genuine labor-market improvement rather than labor-force shrinkage, supporting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The relation should be absent when payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change > 150 and UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger does not outperform baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur less often than negative returns in triggered instances over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low FEDFUNDS level after a tightening phase supports equities only once policy stops rising, because the market then prices a pause in discount-rate pressure. This should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when inflation is not accelerating.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is unchanged or down for 2 consecutive months and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if rate-pause episodes fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal becomes negative during multiple post-tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 combined with weak RSAFS indicates broad demand deterioration that will compress revenue expectations and push the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter. The mechanism is most informative when both series weaken together rather than separately.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth <= 0 and RSAFS 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not followed by below-median SPX 60-day returns, or if the joint-trigger hit rate is not significantly different from zero in walk-forward tests.
